what: Keep the repaired split-K=7 8x64 branch and trim MAX_KSPLIT down to the actual partial count
why: The safer 8x64 reduction line already passed server test, but it still carries padded partial metadata into the reduction stage. Exact MAX_KSPLIT helped earlier split-K reductions by cutting useless zero-partial work, and this isolates that metadata change on the leaderboard-safer valid branch.

what: Revisit the strongest split-K=7 8x32 benchmark branch with exact MAX_KSPLIT metadata
why: The best fixed-input result so far came from the repaired split-K=7 plus 8x32 reduction retile, but the exact-partial metadata variant on that branch was never benchmarked locally. This isolates the same reduction-contract cleanup on the fastest benchmark geometry before spending any new leaderboard quota.

what: Keep the repaired split-K=7 path and narrow only the reduction width to 16x32 with exact MAX_KSPLIT metadata
why: The current width sweep separates 8x64 and 8x32, but the 16x32 control was only staged before quota blocked. Combining that control tile with exact partial-count metadata isolates the effect of the narrower width without the smaller row tile and gives a cleaner comparison against both queued candidates.

what: Benchmark the valid split-K=7 8x64 reduction branch with padded power-of-two MAX_KSPLIT
why: v297 shows the gluon reduce kernel requires MAX_KSPLIT to stay power-of-two, so the exact-count variants are invalid. The right recovery is to return to the already test-passed 8x64 branch with padded metadata and finally get the missing fixed-input benchmark for the safer leaderboard candidate.
override: This intentionally revisits the v296 branch because the exact-count follow-up failed in compilation and the valid padded 8x64 candidate still lacks a benchmark in this session.

what: Benchmark the valid split-K=7 16x32 reduction-width control with padded MAX_KSPLIT
why: After the exact-count failures, the remaining clean comparison is the unmeasured padded 16x32 control: same repaired split-K=7 main kernel, same required power-of-two metadata, and only the reduction width narrowed from 64 to 32 while keeping the default row tile.
override: This revives the previously blocked 16x32 control on the now-confirmed valid padded-metadata contract.

what: Benchmark the valid split-K=7 4x32 reduction control with padded MAX_KSPLIT
why: With exact-count metadata ruled out, the next useful valid neighbor is the more aggressive 4x32 reduction retile on the repaired split-K=7 path. This stays on the required padded power-of-two metadata contract and measures whether more M-parallel reduction helps the large-K split path.
override: This revives the previously staged 4x32 control on the confirmed valid padded-metadata contract after the exact-count detour failed.

what: Benchmark the valid split-K=7 4x16 reduction control with padded MAX_KSPLIT
why: The padded split-K=7 contract is now confirmed by v300 and v301 passing server test. The remaining nearby valid reduction neighbor is 4x16, which increases both M-parallelism and N-parallelism relative to the default reduction and completes the local sweep around the 8x64/16x32/4x32 candidates.
override: This revives the previously prepared 4x16 control after the exact-count branch was ruled out and the padded contract was revalidated.

what: Use a power-of-two split-K=4 on the repaired BSK=256 path with the strong 8x32 reduction tile
why: The upstream gluon reduction kernel is laid out around a power-of-two K-split dimension, and the current split-K=7 branch always runs with a masked final partial plus padded MAX_KSPLIT=8. A real split-K=4 on the same BSK=256 main kernel removes that odd partial entirely while keeping the stronger 8x32 reduction geometry.
override: Earlier split-K work focused on split-2 with older configs and split-8 with invalid BSK=128. This is the untested power-of-two split count on the repaired BSK=256 branch.

what: Use power-of-two split-K=4 on the repaired BSK=256 path with default 16x64 reduction
why: v304 still combines a split-count change with the benchmark-only 8x32 reduction retile. This control keeps the repaired BSK=256 split main kernel but restores the safer default reduction geometry, isolating whether the power-of-two split count itself helps without another leaderboard-unstable reduction change.
override: This is the control pair for the already-staged v304 experiment and isolates split count from reduction retile effects.
